sources_audited.note: The BLS page supports the May 2023 three-digit NAICS 332000 employment figures, including 1,439,340 total jobs, 202,750 other production occupations, 23,760 general office clerks, and engineering-technician categories. It does not itself support the six-digit AI-exposure share or five-year implementation realization; those are disclosed proxy and estimate judgments.
The Census June 2026 release supports the stated 1.427 million annualized housing-start rate, 3.5% year-over-year increase, 1.367 million permit rate, and 2.3% year-over-year decrease.

EPA supports the stated 700-gallon annual savings from replacing inefficient faucets or aerators and about 30% greater efficiency for labeled faucets or aerators. These facts establish an efficiency driver but do not directly quantify five-year NAICS demand or the operator-requirement estimate.

EPA supports more than 45,900 labeled models across several product categories, more than 10,000 labeled homes, and 1.2 trillion gallons saved in 2023. The cross-category model count is not a direct measure of plumbing-trim adoption, replacement volume, or domestic manufacturing demand.

EPA supports that WaterSense products are independently certified for efficiency and performance and are 20% more water-efficient than average category products. It does not directly support the 0.97 operator-requirement estimate or quantify displacement risk.

caveats: The a proxy uses three-digit fabricated-metal occupational counts and an unquantified task mapping; it does not directly observe the six-digit AI-exposable compensation share.
The same BLS occupational page is cited for rho but contains no implementation evidence; rho remains an explicitly disclosed estimate based on workflow judgment.

The d5 bridge combines a single-month construction snapshot with WaterSense efficiency and program-scale facts rather than a five-year, constant-price shipment forecast for NAICS 332913, and the packet appropriately discloses that limitation.
The WaterSense model count spans multiple product categories and should not be read as plumbing-trim adoption or incremental replacement demand.
The o estimate is economically plausible for physical water-control hardware, but its narrow range is not directly measured by the cited efficiency-label pages and remains judgmental.

Eligibility, transfer availability, retention, and implementation realization are unsupported by representative firm or transaction datasets; these weaknesses are disclosed and correctly keep the record MODEL_CONDITIONED.
summary: Mechanics pass and all five cited sources are reachable. The cited numerical claims are accurate, but most source-to-primitive links are broad proxies or contextual support rather than direct measurements, while several decisive inputs are explicit estimates. Those weaknesses do not meet the materiality test: correcting or honestly downgrading the affected support would not move this already STRUCTURAL_OUT base tier or change the VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action. The record is useful and candid enough to publish with caveats.
